summary: Iter19 VALIDATED. Backend proxy now forwards created_by to harness-eval on every affected endpoint (a-k). Pytest /app/backend/tests/test_created_by_passthrough.py: 11/11 PASS using httpx.MockTransport to intercept outbound AsyncClient calls. Frontend: RunEvalModal POST /api/eval/jobs body carries created_by=<USER_ID> alongside group_run_id + evals. /evals and /schedules GET list calls carry ?created_by=<USER_ID>. /datasets + /cortex + /stats + /health show zero created_by leaks.
critical_code_review_comments: proxy_submit_eval at server.py:369 correctly forwards created_by via `if body.get('created_by'): payload['created_by'] = body['created_by']` — validated by mocked httpx.AsyncClient receiving the exact JSON body with created_by.

All GET/DELETE eval-proxy endpoints accept `created_by: Optional[str] = None` query parameter and conditionally add it to forwarded params — validated for jobs list/get/cancel, groups/{id}/jobs, jobs/aggregate, scheduled-batches list/get/delete/runs.
proxy_trigger_scheduled_batch forwards the full POST body (including created_by) as-is via `json=body or {}` — validated.
Nit: harness may 400 on unknown query keys; prior iters report harness is tolerant but worth confirming end-to-end in a staging harness once real traffic flows. No blocker.
Carry-over from iter18: server.py is still ~1180 lines; consider splitting into eval_proxy.py / auth.py modules for maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (RunEvalModal submit -> POST body has created_by; /evals + /schedules GETs carry ?created_by; /datasets zero leaks)

One real eval job was submitted through the UI labeled 'TEST_iter19_created_by-2026-04-30T18-00-05-204Z-zpny' as part of the end-to-end validation. context_for_next_testing_agent: To re-validate iter19: (1) Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_created_by_passthrough.py -v` — all 11 must pass. (2) Seed acm_session_token, open /evals, click [data-testid='new-eval-btn'], click a problem row by text, click [data-testid='eval-next-step'], fill Group Run ID via placeholder 'e.g. nightly, sonnet-vs-opus', click [data-testid='eval-next-step'], click [data-testid='submit-eval-button']. Assert POST /api/eval/jobs body JSON has created_by=<user_id>. If session token expired, re-seed via /app/memory/test_credentials.md steps.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (now fixed): The backend proxy endpoints in /app/backend/server.py were not declaring created_by in their signatures/payload construction. proxy_submit_eval was rebuilding the outbound payload with an explicit allowlist of keys (user_id, group_run_id, agent_name, evals) and silently dropping created_by even when the frontend attachOwnership interceptor injected it into the request body. Similarly all GET/DELETE handlers had no created_by query param so it was discarded at the proxy layer. Fix applied by main agent: (a) proxy_submit_eval now has `if body.get('created_by'): payload['created_by'] = body['created_by']`; (b) every affected GET/DELETE handler now declares `created_by: Optional[str] = None` and conditionally injects it into outbound params; (c) proxy_trigger_scheduled_batch forwards the body wholesale. Verified via httpx.MockTransport intercept — outbound request to EVAL_API_BASE/api/v1/... carries created_by in the correct location (body for POSTs, query for GETs/DELETE) for all 11 endpoints. Frontend DevTools also confirm the complete round-trip from RunEvalModal through to the internal API.
